NFeMiner Package

nfeminer.classification module

class nfeminer.classification.NFeMinerGTINEstimator(batch: DataFrame = None, model: str = 'all-MiniLM-L6-v2', n_threads: int = 0, force_cpu: bool = False, min_ngram_confidence: float = 0.5, min_embedding_confidence: float = 0.4, basedir='./')[source]

Bases: object

A class to estimate GTIN codes from product descriptions using multiple matching models (string matching, n-gram vectorization, and sentence embeddings).

accuracy_measure(mask)[source]

Measures and prints accuracy for a subset of classified rows.

Parameters:

mask (Union[list, pd.Series]) – Boolean mask or list of row indices.

embedding_classifier()[source]

Classifies using sentence embeddings and 1-NN with Euclidean distance.

ngram_classifier()[source]

Classifies using n-gram vectorization and 1-NN with Euclidean distance.

report()[source]

Generates a report of the DataFrame results, including GTIN coverage and a summary of unique combinations.

This method checks if the results DataFrame is empty or None. If not, it prints:

  • Total number of rows in the dataset

  • Total number of rows with non-null GTIN

  • Percentage of rows with GTIN

Then, it returns a DataFrame summarizing unique combinations of ‘original’, ‘description_ref’, ‘gtin’, ‘similarity’, and ‘method’, including their counts, sorted by similarity.

Returns:

A DataFrame containing unique combinations of

’original’, ‘description_ref’, ‘gtin’, ‘similarity’, and ‘method’, with a ‘count’ column and sorted by ‘similarity’.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

string_matching_classifier()[source]

Classifies using direct string-matching dictionary.

class nfeminer.classification.NFeMinerModelCreator(data: DataFrame, trusted_records: int = 100, vectorizer: str = 'TFIDF_CHAR_NGRAM', embedding: str = 'SBERT', basedir='./')[source]

Bases: object

NFeModelCreator is a class designed to build multiple text-based models for product description data.

It supports different vectorization strategies and embedding models to create: - A string match dictionary model for exact matching of unique product descriptions per GTIN. - A vectorizer-based 1-NN classification model with PyTorch tensors. - A Sentence-BERT embedding model with tensor representations for semantic similarity.

dataset

Input dataset containing GTIN and original product descriptions.

Type:

pd.DataFrame

trusted_records

Threshold for minimum record count to consider a GTIN reliable.

Type:

int

vectorizer

Text vectorizer for feature extraction.

Type:

sklearn Pipeline or Vectorizer

embedding

Sentence transformer model for embedding text.

Type:

SentenceTransformer

Raises:

ValueError – If the input dataset is empty or if unsupported vectorizer or embedding is specified.

create_embedding_tensors_model()[source]

Create an embedding-based tensor model using Sentence-BERT.

Steps: - Filter dataset to remove null descriptions. - Group by GTIN, selecting most frequent original description. - Encode descriptions into embeddings (tensor). - Store tensor embeddings with GTIN and original descriptions in a DataFrame. - Save DataFrame as a pickle file.

create_file_path(filename: str = 'filename.pickle') → str[source]

Helper function to create directory and file path for saving models.

Parameters:

  • dir (str) – Directory where model files are stored.

  • filename (str) – Name of the file to save.

Returns:

Full file path to save the model.

Return type:

str

create_string_match_model()[source]

Create a dictionary-based string match model.

This model identifies unique ‘original’ product descriptions per GTIN that are frequent enough (above trusted_records threshold) and exclusive (do not appear in other GTINs).

The result is saved as a pickle file mapping unique descriptions to their GTIN.

create_vectorizer_tensors_model()[source]

Create a vectorizer-based model for 1-NN classification.

Steps: - Filter dataset to remove null descriptions. - Group by GTIN, selecting most frequent original description. - Train selected vectorizer on these descriptions. - Save vectorizer model. - Convert feature vectors to PyTorch tensors. - Store tensor vectors with GTIN and original descriptions in a DataFrame. - Save DataFrame as a pickle file.

get_unique_descriptions(row, all_data) → set[source]

Helper function to identify unique ‘original’ descriptions for a given GTIN.

Parameters:

  • row (pd.Series) – Row containing ‘gtin’ and set of ‘original’ descriptions.

  • all_data (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame with all grouped data.

Returns:

Set of descriptions unique to the GTIN in the given row.

Return type:

set

nfeminer.clustering module

class nfeminer.clustering.NFeCluster(edge_generator: Any, threshold: float = 0.0)[source]

Bases: object

Orchestrator for clustering using a precomputed compact graph from an EdgeGenerator.

The class is intentionally small: it asks the EdgeGenerator for a compact igraph.Graph (unique representatives only) at initialization, then exposes run() to execute a chosen igraph community detection algorithm and expands the compact labels back to the original IDs using edge_generator.expand_clusters(…).

Example

gen = StringMatchEdgeGenerator(items, ids, cache_dir) clusterer = NFeCluster(edge_generator=gen, threshold=0.85)

# Run Louvain expanded = clusterer.run(“louvain”, resolution=1.0) # expanded is list[(original_id, params_with_cluster), …] length == len(ids)

Parameters:

  • edge_generator – Instance implementing generate_compact_graph(threshold) and expand_clusters(compact_labels, params).

  • threshold – Threshold used to filter edges when generating the compact graph.

run(algorithm: str, **kwargs) → List[Tuple[int, Dict[str, Any]]][source]

Run the requested clustering algorithm on the compact graph, then expand results.

Parameters:

  • algorithm – name of the algorithm to run. Supported names: - “louvain” - “leiden” - “label_propagation” - “walktrap” - “fastgreedy” - “infomap” - “connected_components”

  • **kwargs – extra parameters forwarded to the underlying igraph method when applicable.

Returns:

list with length equal to the total number of original IDs; each entry contains the original ID and a dict with metadata + assigned cluster (the cluster value is assigned inside edge_generator.expand_clusters and will be a UUID per cluster).

Return type:

List[Tuple[original_id, params_with_cluster]]

nfeminer.elasticsearch module

class nfeminer.elasticsearch.ElasticSearchDocumentService(client)[source]

Bases: object

Manages document operations in Elasticsearch, including indexing, updating, deleting, and searching.

count_documents(index_name: str) → int[source]

Count the documents in Elasticsearch.

Parameters:

index_name (str) – The name of the index.

Return type:

int

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the search operation.

delete_document(index_name: str, doc_id: str) → Dict[str, str][source]

Deletes a document from Elasticsearch.

Parameters:

  • index_name (str) – The name of the index.

  • doc_id (str) – The document ID to delete.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the delete result message.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the delete operation.

index_documents(index_name: str, documents: Dict | List[Dict], batch_size: int = 500, progress_callback=None) → Dict[str, str][source]

Indexes documents into Elasticsearch in batches, ignoring documents that raise errors.

Parameters:

  • index_name (str) – Name of the Elasticsearch index.

  • documents (Union[Dict, List[Dict]]) – A single document (dict) or a list of documents (list[dict]).

  • batch_size (int) – Number of documents to send per batch.

Returns:

A message indicating the number of indexed documents.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

  • ValueError – If the input data is not in the expected format.

  • RuntimeError – If an error occurs during indexing (other than individual document errors).

search_documents(index_name: str, query: Dict) → Dict[str, List[Dict] | str][source]

Searches for documents in Elasticsearch based on a query.

Parameters:

  • index_name (str) – The name of the index.

  • query (Dict) – A dictionary containing the search query.

Returns:

A dictionary with the search results. The key ‘hits’ contains a list of documents, or an error message if no results were found.

Return type:

Dict[str, Union[List[Dict], str]]

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the search operation.

update_document(index_name: str, doc_id: str, update_fields: Dict) → Dict[str, str][source]

Updates an existing document in Elasticsearch.

Parameters:

  • index_name (str) – The name of the index.

  • doc_id (str) – The document ID to update.

  • update_fields (Dict) – The fields to update in the document.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the update result message.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the update operation.

class nfeminer.elasticsearch.ElasticSearchIndexService(client: Elasticsearch)[source]

Bases: object

Manages Elasticsearch index operations, including creation, updating, and deletion.

check_index_exists(index_name: str) → Dict[str, str][source]

Check if an Elasticsearch index exists and return a message.

This function checks if the specified index exists in Elasticsearch and returns a message indicating whether the index is present or not.

Parameters:

index_name (str) – The name of the index to check.

Returns:

A dictionary containing a message that indicates if the index

exists or not.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs while checking the index’s existence.

create_index(index_name: str, mapping: str | Dict) → Dict[str, str][source]

Creates an index in Elasticsearch with a provided mapping.

Parameters:

  • index_name (str) – The name of the index to create.

  • mapping (Union[str, Dict]) – The mapping definition as a dictionary or a JSON string.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the index creation result message.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

  • ValueError – If mapping is invalid.

  • RuntimeError – If an error occurs during index creation.

delete_index(index_name: str) → Dict[str, str][source]

Deletes an entire index from Elasticsearch.

Parameters:

index_name (str) – The name of the index to delete.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the index deletion result message.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during the index deletion operation.

update_index(index_name: str, settings: Dict) → Dict[str, str][source]

Updates an existing Elasticsearch index with new settings.

Parameters:

  • index_name (str) – The name of the index to update.

  • settings (Dict) – The new settings to apply to the index.

Returns:

A dictionary containing the index update result message.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

RuntimeError – If an error occurs during index update.

class nfeminer.elasticsearch.NFeMinerElasticSearch[source]

Bases: object

Facade for managing Elasticsearch operations related to NFe (Nota Fiscal Eletrônica) processing.

This class provides a high-level interface to Elasticsearch for Brazilian electronic invoice (NFe) use cases. It abstracts both document-level and index-level operations, making it easier to manage indexing, updating, deletion, and search of structured fiscal data.

Parameters:

  • host (str) – Hostname or IP address of the Elasticsearch server. Default is “localhost”.

  • port (int | str) – Port number for Elasticsearch. Default is 9200.

  • scheme (str) – Protocol scheme (“http” or “https”). Default is “http”.

client

The raw Elasticsearch client instance.

Type:

Elasticsearch

document_service

Document-level operations manager.

Type:

ElasticSearchDocumentService

index_service

Index-level operations manager.

Type:

ElasticSearchIndexService

get_client() → Elasticsearch[source]

Returns the underlying Elasticsearch client.

get_client() → Elasticsearch[source]

Returns the Elasticsearch client.

Returns:

The Elasticsearch client instance.

Return type:

Elasticsearch

nfeminer.enrichment module

class nfeminer.enrichment.NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel[source]

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for models designed to extract structured data from electronic invoice (NFe) descriptions.

This class defines the expected prompt structure, output format, and a standard method interface for generating enriched product data in JSON format. Subclasses must implement the generate method.

Class Attributes:

instruction (str): Instructions given to the model describing the expected behavior and output structure. prompt_template (str): Template for composing the full prompt to be sent to the model. input_template (str): Template for formatting invoice and item fields into a prompt for the model. example (str): An example of a valid model response for reference.

generate(text

str) -> str: Abstract method. Should generate a raw response given a prompt.

json_generate(invoice_id, item_id, ncm_code, gtin_code, sales_unit, quantity_sold, unit_price, description) → dict[source]

Generates enriched JSON data from basic product and invoice fields.

example = '**Exemplo de saída:**\n\n```json\n{\n  "produto": {\n    "id_item": "1",\n    "id_nfe": "1234567890",\n    "ncm": "01012100",\n    "gtin": "7891234567890",\n    "descricao": {\n      "original": "CAIXA C/ 12 GARRAFAS AGUA 500ML",\n      "enriquecida": {\n        "produto_base": "Água Mineral",\n        "produto_detalhado": "Uma caixa com 12 garrafas de 500 ml de água mineral",\n        "detalhes_extraidos": {\n          "embalagem": {\n            "tipo": "Caixa",\n            "quantidade": {\n              "valor": 12,\n              "unidade": "unidades"\n            }\n          },\n          "caracteristicas": {\n            "dimensoes": null,\n            "peso": null,\n            "volume_por_unidade": {\n              "valor": 500,\n              "unidade": "mL"\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        "informacoes_adicionais": {\n          "marca": null,\n          "origem": "Nacional",\n          "categoria": ["Bebidas", "água", "água mineral sem gas"]\n        }\n      },\n      "tags": ["água mineral", "caixa de 12 garrafas de 500ml"]\n    },\n    "unidade_comercializacao": "UN",\n    "quantidade_comercializada": {\n      "valor": 24,\n      "unidade": "unidades"\n    },\n    "quantidade_convertida": {\n      "valor": 12,\n      "unidade": "caixas"\n    },\n    "valor_unitario": {\n      "valor": 2.50,\n      "moeda": "BRL"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```'
abstractmethod generate(text: str) → str[source]

Generates a raw model response based on the given input text.

This method must be implemented by any subclass using a specific model architecture (e.g., local model, OpenAI API, Ollama, etc.).

Parameters:

text (str) – Input text (prompt) to send to the model.

Returns:

The generated text response from the model.

Return type:

str

input_template = 'ID NFe: {invoice_id}\nID Item: {item_id}\nNomenclatura Comum do Mercosul (NCM): {ncm_code}\nGlobal Trade Item Number (GTIN): {gtin_code}\nUnidade de Comercialização: {sales_unit}\nQuantidade Comercializada: {quantity_sold}\nValor Unitário: {unit_price}\nDescrição: {description}'
instruction = 'Você é um modelo de linguagem treinado para processar dados de notas fiscais eletrônicas (NFEs) em português. Sua tarefa é analisar a descrição do item e outros campos fornecidos, extrair informações estruturadas e retornar um JSON formatado conforme uma estrutura predefinida.\n\n**Regras importantes:**\n1. As notas fiscais estão em português.\n2. O JSON de saída deve ser formatado conforme a estrutura detalhada abaixo.\n3. Certifique-se de separar números de suas unidades para campos como quantidade, peso, volume, etc.\n4. Utilize informações presentes na descrição para preencher campos específicos no JSON, enriquecendo os dados sempre que possível.\n5. Se um campo não puder ser inferido, deixe-o vazio ou use `null`.\n\n---\n\n**Estrutura esperada do JSON de saída:**\n\n```json\n{\n  "produto": {\n    "id_item": "<ID único do item>",\n    "id_nfe": "<ID único da nota fiscal>",\n    "ncm": "<Código NCM>",\n    "gtin": "<Código GTIN>",\n    "descricao": {\n      "original": "<Descrição original extraída da nota fiscal>",\n      "enriquecida": {\n        "produto_base": "<Nome base do produto>",\n        "produto_detalhado": "<Descrição detalhada inferida do produto>",\n        "detalhes_extraidos": {\n          "embalagem": {\n            "tipo": "<Tipo de embalagem (ex.: caixa, saco, garrafa)>",\n            "quantidade": {\n              "valor": "<Número>",\n              "unidade": "<Unidade (ex.: unidades, litros, kg)>"\n            }\n          },\n          "caracteristicas": {\n            "dimensoes": {\n              "valor": "<Número>",\n              "unidade": "<Unidade (ex.: cm, m, litros)>"\n            },\n            "peso": {\n              "valor": "<Número>",\n              "unidade": "<Unidade (ex.: gramas, kg)>"\n            },\n            "volume_por_unidade": {\n              "valor": "<Número>",\n              "unidade": "<Unidade (ex.: mL, litros)>"\n            }\n          }\n        },\n        "informacoes_adicionais": {\n          "marca": "<Marca do produto, se disponível>",\n          "origem": "<Origem (ex.: nacional, importado)>",\n          "categoria": ["Categoria hierarquica do produto - categoria genérica", "categoria intermediaria", "categoria especifica"]\n        }\n      },\n      "tags": [\n        "tag semantica 1",\n        "tag semantica 2",\n        "tag semantica 3",\n        "tag semantica 4",\n        "tag semantica 5"\n      ]\n    },\n    "unidade_comercializacao": "<Unidade de comercialização>",\n    "quantidade_comercializada": {\n      "valor": "<Número>",\n      "unidade": "<Unidade>"\n    },\n    "quantidade_convertida": {\n      "valor": "<Número convertido>",\n      "unidade": "<Unidade convertida>"\n    },\n    "valor_unitario": {\n      "valor": "<Valor unitário>",\n      "moeda": "<Código da moeda (ex.: BRL, USD)>"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n'
json_generate(invoice_id: str, item_id: str, ncm_code: str, gtin_code: str, sales_unit: str, quantity_sold: float, unit_price: float, description: str) → dict[source]

Generates a structured JSON representation of an invoice item using the language model.

This method constructs a prompt using invoice and product details, invokes the model, and parses the response into a validated JSON structure.

Parameters:

  • invoice_id (str) – Unique identifier of the electronic invoice (NFe).

  • item_id (str) – Identifier of the item within the invoice.

  • ncm_code (str) – Mercosur Common Nomenclature (NCM) code used for tax classification.

  • gtin_code (str) – Global Trade Item Number (GTIN), also known as the barcode.

  • sales_unit (str) – Unit of measurement used for selling the item (e.g., ‘UN’, ‘kg’).

  • quantity_sold (float) – Quantity of the item sold.

  • unit_price (float) – Unit price of the item.

  • description (str) – Raw item description from the invoice.

Returns:

Validated structured JSON dictionary containing enriched product data.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If the model output cannot be parsed as valid JSON or have a incorrect format.

prompt_template = '{}\n{}'
class nfeminer.enrichment.NFeMinerJSONValidator(*, produto: _Product)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Top-level Pydantic schema used for validating enriched output.

produto

Full product structure.

Type:

_Product

model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

produto: _Product
class nfeminer.enrichment.NFeMinerLocalModel(model_name: str = 'models/gemma3_12b-pt_hf')[source]

Bases: NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel

Local implementation of a fine-tuned language model for structured NFe item extraction.

This class loads a model (typically fine-tuned using NFeFinetuner) and applies it to generate structured JSON outputs from textual descriptions of items in Brazilian electronic invoices (NFes). It uses a standard instruction and formatting template defined in the base class to create consistent prompts.

model

The loaded fine-tuned language model.

Type:

PreTrainedModel

tokenizer

Tokenizer associated with the loaded model.

Type:

PreTrainedTokenizer

pipe

Hugging Face pipeline for deterministic text generation.

Type:

transformers.Pipeline

Parameters:

model_name (str, optional) – Path to the local model directory. Defaults to “models/gemma3_12b-pt_hf”.

generate(text

str) -> str: Generates structured output from a single input text using the model.

generate(text: str) → str[source]

Generates a structured JSON response based on the input text using the local model.

The input is formatted with an instruction and passed through the generation pipeline to produce a completion suitable for NFe data extraction.

Parameters:

text (str) – Input prompt representing an item description from an NFe.

Returns:

The generated JSON-formatted string response from the model.

Return type:

str

class nfeminer.enrichment.NFeMinerOllamaModel(model_name, host='http://localhost:11434', options={'num_predict': 16384, 'repeat_penalty': 1.1, 'seed': 777, 'temperature': 1, 'top_k': 64, 'top_p': 0.95})[source]

Bases: NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel

Implementation of the NFeMiner interface using a locally hosted Ollama model.

This class connects to an Ollama server to run a specified local language model for processing electronic invoice item descriptions and generating structured enrichment.

model_name

Name of the model to be used via Ollama (must be pulled beforehand or automatically).

Type:

str

client

Client used to communicate with the Ollama server.

Type:

ollama.Client

options

Generation parameters passed to the model (e.g., temperature, top_k, etc.).

Type:

dict

Parameters:

  • model_name (str) – Name of the Ollama model to use (e.g., “llama3”, “mistral-nfe”).

  • host (str, optional) – URL of the Ollama server. Defaults to “http://localhost:11434”.

  • options (dict, optional) – Generation options for model behavior. Includes temperature, top_k, etc. Defaults to reasonable values for deterministic NFe inference.

generate(text

str) -> str: Sends a prompt to the Ollama model and returns the generated response.

generate(text: str) → str[source]

Generates a response from the Ollama-hosted local model based on the provided input.

Constructs a formatted prompt using instruction and example templates, then sends it to the Ollama model with the specified generation options.

Parameters:

text (str) – The input text to be processed by the model.

Returns:

The response generated by the Ollama model.

Return type:

str

nfeminer.finetuning module

class nfeminer.finetuning.NFeFinetuner(df: DataFrame, config: dict)[source]

Bases: object

Handles fine-tuning of a model on NF-e data using configuration parameters.

The configuration dictionary should include the following keys:

  • “FastModel.from_pretrained”: Parameters to load the base model.

  • “FastModel.get_peft_model”: Parameters to configure PEFT (Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning).

  • “SFTConfig”: Training configuration parameters.

  • “dataset_path”: Path to the dataset (optional, not used here).

  • “model_output_path”: Path to save the trained model.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – DataFrame with ‘prompt’ and ‘json’ columns.

  • config (dict) – Dictionary containing model and training configurations.

finetune(progress_callback=None)[source]

Runs the full fine-tuning pipeline: training followed by saving.

Parameters:

progress_callback (Callable[[float], None], optional) – Function to receive progress updates. Defaults to None.

Returns:

Training statistics.

Return type:

dict

save()[source]

Saves the model and tokenizer to the configured output path.

Saves both the regular model and a merged version (e.g., for Hugging Face Hub compatibility).

train(progress_callback=None)[source]

Trains the model using the provided dataset and configuration.

Parameters:

progress_callback (Callable[[float], None], optional) – Function to receive progress updates. Defaults to None.

Returns:

Training statistics returned by the Trainer.

Return type:

dict

class nfeminer.finetuning.ProgressCallback(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any)[source]

Bases: TrainerCallback

Custom callback to report training progress.

This callback calculates the training progress as a float between 0.0 and 1.0 and passes it to a user-defined function.

Parameters:

progress_bar_callback (Callable[[float], None]) – A function to receive progress updates.

on_step_end(args, state, control, **kwargs)[source]

Called at the end of each training step.

Parameters:

  • args – Training arguments.

  • state – Current state of the Trainer.

  • control – Trainer control flow object.

  • **kwargs – Additional unused keyword arguments.

nfeminer.similarity_graph module

class nfeminer.similarity_graph.EdgeGenerator(items: List[str], ids: List[int], cache_path: str, similarity_dtype: str = 'float16', max_workers: int | None = None, batch_size: int = 50000)[source]

Bases: ABC

Base class that computes pairwise similarities between unique normalized texts, caches them in LMDB and builds igraph graphs filtered by a threshold.

Subclasses must implement _compute_similarity(a, b) or choose a compare_name that points into the _COMPARE_REGISTRY above.

Example

gen = StringMatchEdgeGenerator(items, ids, cache_dir) args, graph = gen.generate_graph(threshold=0.85)

Parameters:

  • items – list[str] containing text values (may contain duplicates).

  • ids – list[int] same length as items, original integer PRIMARY KEYs (int64).

  • cache_path – directory where LMDB files are stored.

  • similarity_dtype – ‘float16’ or ‘float32’ (default ‘float16’).

  • max_workers – number of parallel workers; default = os.cpu_count().

  • batch_size – internal batch size for writing to LMDB & memory (default 50k).

expand_clusters(compact_labels, params)[source]

Expand compact graph cluster labels back to all original IDs.

Parameters:

  • compact_labels (List[int]) – Cluster label for each representative node (length = n_unique).

  • params (Dict[str, Any]) – Additional metadata (algorithm name, threshold, hyperparameters). A ‘cluster’ field will be added automatically for each id.

Returns:

One entry per original ID: (id_original, params_with_cluster)

Return type:

List[Tuple[int, Dict[str, Any]]]

generate_compact_graph(threshold: float = 0.0) → Graph[source]

Generate a compact igraph.Graph containing only unique texts (representatives).

The method reads pairwise similarities from the LMDB cache (stored between representative ids), applies the provided threshold and builds an igraph that has n_unique vertices. Each vertex receives: - “rep_id”: representative original id chosen for the unique text - “text”: the normalized unique text

Edges are created only between unique representatives whose cached similarity >= threshold and the edge attribute “weight” receives the similarity.

Parameters:

threshold – float in [0,1]. Only edges with similarity >= threshold will be created in the compact graph.

Returns:

compact graph with n_unique vertices and weighted edges.

Return type:

igraph.Graph

class nfeminer.similarity_graph.StringMatchEdgeGenerator(items: List[str], ids: List[int], cache_path: str, similarity_dtype: str = 'float16', max_workers: int | None = None, batch_size: int = 50000)[source]

Bases: EdgeGenerator

Edge generator using difflib.SequenceMatcher as similarity measure.

This class does not need to override the heavy-lifting caching logic; it only provides the comparison name which the worker uses.

COMPARE_NAME = 'sequence'
EDGE_TYPE = 'string_match'
nfeminer.similarity_graph.sequence_compare(a: str, b: str) → float[source]

Compare two strings using difflib.SequenceMatcher.ratio().

Module contents

class nfeminer.NFeMiner(model: NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel, index_file_path: str, index_name='nfe_products')[source]

Bases: object

Facade for semantic enrichment of NFe (Brazilian electronic invoice) items using a pluggable language model.

This class wraps an instance of a language model compatible with the NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel interface and extends its functionality with GTIN estimation, product clustering, and integration with Elasticsearch for indexing and searching enriched data.

model

The language model used for generating structured outputs.

Type:

NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel

elasticsearch

Interface for indexing and searching enriched documents.

Type:

NFeMinerElasticSearch

estimator

Classifier for predicting GTIN from item descriptions.

Type:

NFeMinerGTINEstimator

clusterizer

Component for clustering product descriptions.

Type:

NFeCluster

Parameters:

  • model (NFeMinerBaseGenerateModel) – Instance of a compatible model that implements json_generate.

  • index_file_path (str) – Path to a JSON or CSV file containing products to index. If None, indexing is skipped.

  • index_name (str, optional) – Name of the Elasticsearch index to use. Defaults to “nfe_products”.

clustering(descriptions: List[str], index: List, tmp_files_path='./') → dict[source]

Clusters product descriptions based on semantic similarity using a graph-based community detection algorithm.

This method constructs a similarity graph from the provided descriptions using StringMatchEdgeGenerator, applies a graph clustering via NFeCluster, and returns the expanded cluster assignments mapped to the original IDs.

Parameters:

  • descriptions (List[str]) – List of product descriptions.

  • index (List) – List of original IDs associated with each description. Must be the same length as descriptions. These IDs are used to expand cluster labels back to the full dataset.

  • tmp_files_path (str, optional) – Directory where LMDB cache files will be stored. Defaults to “./”. The caching mechanism enables large-scale pairwise similarity computation using limited memory.

Returns:

A dictionary mapping each cluster label (int) to the list of product descriptions assigned to that cluster.

Return type:

dict[int, list[str]]

Notes

  • Similarity is computed using difflib.SequenceMatcher via

StringMatchEdgeGenerator. - Clustering is performed using the Louvain community detection algorithm. - Similarity values are stored with dtype float16 to reduce memory usage.

enrichment(invoice_id: str, item_id: str, ncm_code: str, gtin_code: str, sales_unit: str, quantity_sold: float, unit_price: float, description: str) → dict[source]

Performs semantic enrichment using the model with structured input fields.

Parameters:

  • invoice_id (str) – Unique identifier of the electronic invoice (NFe).

  • item_id (str) – Identifier of the item within the invoice.

  • ncm_code (str) – Mercosur Common Nomenclature (NCM) code for tax classification.

  • gtin_code (str) – Global Trade Item Number (GTIN), e.g., barcode.

  • sales_unit (str) – Unit of measure used for selling the item (e.g., “kg”, “pcs”).

  • quantity_sold (float) – Quantity of the item sold.

  • unit_price (float) – Price per unit of the item.

  • description (str) – Natural language description of the product.

Returns:

Structured enrichment output returned by the model.

Return type:

dict

enrichment_and_index(raw_data: Dict | List[Dict], batch_size: int = 500) → Dict[str, str][source]

Performs enrichment on raw input data and indexes the result into Elasticsearch.

Parameters:

  • raw_data (Union[Dict, List[Dict]]) – Single or list of dictionaries containing input fields.

  • batch_size (int, optional) – Number of documents to index per batch. Defaults to 500.

Returns:

Dictionary with success message and document count.

Return type:

Dict[str, str]

Raises:

ValueError – If the input format is invalid or required fields are missing.

gtin_estimator(training_description: list[str], training_gtin: list[str], classify_descriptions: list[str]) → List[dict][source]

Predict GTIN values for multiple product descriptions.

Parameters:

  • training_description (list[str]) – List of training descriptions used to train the model. Example: training_description_gtin = [‘Carne bovina’,’Carne porco’]

  • training_gtin (list[str]) – List of training gtin used to train the model. Example: training_gtin = [‘465465’,’4654564’]

  • classify_descriptions (list[str]) –

    List of unlabeled product descriptions for classification. .. rubric:: Example

    classify_descriptions = [‘carne bovina alcatra’, ‘porco dianteiro’]

Returns:

List of dictionaries containing GTIN classification results.

Return type:

List[dict]

search_all() → dict[source]

Retrieves all documents stored in the index.

Returns:

All indexed documents.

Return type:

dict

search_combined(must: List[dict] = [], should: List[dict] = [], must_not: List[dict] = []) → dict[source]

Executes a boolean query with must, should, and must_not clauses.

Parameters:

  • must (List[dict]) – Conditions that must be satisfied.

  • should (List[dict]) – Optional conditions that boost relevance.

  • must_not (List[dict]) – Conditions that must be excluded.

Returns:

Search results from Elasticsearch.

Return type:

dict

search_numeric_range(field: str, gte: float | None = None, lte: float | None = None) → dict[source]

Filters documents where a numeric field falls within a given range.

Parameters:

  • field (str) – Field name to filter.

  • gte (float, optional) – Minimum value (inclusive).

  • lte (float, optional) – Maximum value (inclusive).

Returns:

Matching documents.

Return type:

dict

search_numeric_term(field: str, value: int | float) → dict[source]

Searches for documents with an exact numeric value in the specified field.

Parameters:

  • field (str) – Field name to filter.

  • value (int | float) – Exact value to match.

Returns:

Matching documents.

Return type:

dict

search_string(value: str) → dict[source]

Performs full-text search across all fields in the index.

Parameters:

value (str) – Search string.

Returns:

Search results from Elasticsearch.

Return type:

dict
